title: trifolium flavum
intro: Trifolium flavum, also known as the yellow clover, is a perennial herb belonging to the legume family and is native to Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The yellow clover is also known to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating symptoms of menopausal hot flashes, and providing relief from respiratory issues like bronchitis. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists for its versatility and effectiveness.
